learning_objectives: Apply psychoanalytic, behavioral, cognitive-behavioral, and humanistic theories in nursing care.
Incorporate milieu therapy principles into practice.

sections.title: Overview of Conceptual Models
sections.content: Psychiatric nursing draws on multiple theories to explain behavior and guide interventions. Understanding these models helps nurses select appropriate therapeutic approaches.

sections.title: Psychoanalytic Theory
sections.content: Freud’s model describes the id, ego, and superego along with unconscious processes that shape personality. Insight-oriented therapy explores conflicts and defense mechanisms.

sections.title: Erikson’s Developmental Stages
sections.content: Erikson built on psychodynamic ideas to outline eight stages across the lifespan. Each stage presents tasks that influence identity and relationships.

sections.title: Peplau’s Interpersonal Theory
sections.content: Peplau emphasized the nurse–client relationship as a vehicle for healing. Her phases—orientation, working, and termination—guide communication and collaboration.

sections.title: Behavioral Therapy Principles
sections.content: Behavioral approaches use conditioning and reinforcement to change maladaptive actions. Techniques include systematic desensitization and behavior modification.

sections.title: Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy
sections.content: CBT combines cognitive restructuring with behavioral practice to address problematic thoughts and actions. It is evidence-based for many disorders.

sections.title: Humanistic Approach
sections.content: Humanistic theories, such as Rogers’ person-centered therapy, focus on self-actualization and unconditional positive regard to foster growth.

sections.title: Milieu Therapy
sections.content: Milieu therapy uses the therapeutic environment itself as an agent of change. Structured routines, roles, and social interactions promote safety and skill building.

sections.title: Integrating Models in Nursing Practice
sections.content: Nurses often blend concepts from several theories to meet individual needs. Knowing each model’s strengths allows flexible, patient-centered care.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.content: A working knowledge of major theories equips nurses to choose interventions that support recovery across diverse clinical situations.
